commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=53802c6a93f654ae0d97c82fd0b3bef08e6bddf8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch disables tar hardlink emission during backup creation, records Link entry metadata during backup verification, rejects unsafe hardlink targets, adds backup regression tests, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. The source-level path is high confidence: create a backup from a tree with hardlinked files where tar emits a Link entry with a traversal-bearing linkpath; current main remaps entry.path and verify ignores linkpath, though I did not run macOS bsdtar in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and a focused verifier fix; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup evidence for them.

target.title: fix(backup): dereference archive hardlinks
target.body:
## Summary
- disable tar hardlink emission during backup creation so hardlinked files are archived as regular entries
- include hardlink targets in backup verification and reject traversal/absolute targets
- add regression coverage for hardlinked backup payloads and unsafe Link headers

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/backup-create.ts src/infra/backup-create.test.ts src/commands/backup-verify.ts src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.ts src/commands/backup.test.ts src/commands/backup.create-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.atomic.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/backup-create.test.ts src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.test.ts src/commands/backup.create-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.atomic.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live macOS bsdtar restore smoke; this environment is Linux.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#54242
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
